'''Hakim Miloudi''' (born 26 June 1993) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] in the [[Super XIII]], and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He previously played for [[Saint-Esteve XIII Catalan|Saint-Esteve]], [[AS Carcassonne]] in France and for Canadian club [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Super League]]. Miloudi has also played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

==Background==
Miloudi was born in [[Perpignan]], [[France]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Miloudi began his career at [[Saint-Esteve XIII Catalan]] and played in the [[Elite One Championship]] for [[AS Carcassonne]]. He represented France at junior international level, winning man of the match for France u18s in a victory over Australian Schoolboys.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/other/report?RLE00000295 |title=RLEF |access-date=2018-04-02 |archive-date=2018-04-03 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180403112521/http://www.rlef.eu.com/other/report?RLE00000295 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
In 2017 Miloudi joined [[Hull F.C.]] on trial and later signed a two-year contract.<ref>[http://www.rugby-league.com/superleague/article/50818/hull-snap-up-trialist-miloudi-and-tie-down-young-pair-to-new-deals Hull snap up trialist Miloudi and tie down young pair to new deals]</ref> He was then sent on loan to [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]. While playing for Doncaster in 2018 he tied the club record for most points scored in a match, scoring 32 points in a victory over [[Coventry Bears]].<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.doncasterfreepress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-rlfc-70-coventry-bears-10-hakim-miloudi-equals-dons-points-in-a-game-record-in-big-opening-day-win-1-9022957| title = Doncaster RLFC 70 Coventry Bears 10: Hakim Miloudi equals Dons' points-in-a-game record in big opening day win {{!}} Doncaster Free Press| date = 18 February 2018}}</ref>

On 2 April 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Hull F.C.]] against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=Hull F.C. v Wakefield Trinity|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Palau XIII Broncos===
On 16 October 2020, it was reported that Miloudi had signed for [[Palau XIII Broncos]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|date=16 October 2020|title=Ex-Hull FC man Hakim Miloudi finds new club after Toronto Wolfpack uncertainty|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hakim-miloudi-new-club-hull-4611864|website=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=10 Jan 2021}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 10 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 May 2021|accessdate=12 May 2021|title=Barrow pull off Hakim Miloudi coup|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/barrow-pull-off-hakim-miloudi-coup/}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 1 Jan 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Jan 2024|title=Dual-code international sees Super League return confirmed ahead of new season: ‘A great addition for us’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/dual-code-international-sees-super-league-return-confirmed-ahead-of-new-season-a-great-addition-for-us}}</ref>

===Baroudeurs de Pia XIII===
On 25 Sep 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] in the [[Super XIII]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=|author=Callum Walker|title=London Broncos outgoing star Hakim Miloudi set to join new club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/london-broncos-outgoing-star-hakim-miloudi-set-to-join-new-club/}}</ref>

==International career==
Miloudi made his debut for France in their pre-tournament match against {{rlnt|Jamaica}}. He was named in France's squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]] but was dropped for reportedly missing the bus to the airport.<ref>{{cite web|last=Smailes|first=James|date=15 October 2017|title=Hull FC's Hakim Miloudi kicked out of France World Cup squad|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/hull-fcs-hakim-miloudi-kicked-630023|work=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=1 May 2021}}</ref> He returned to the French team in 2018 for their match against {{rlnt|England}} and the subsequent [[2018 Rugby League European Championship|2018 European Championship]].{{citation needed|date=January 2024}}

Miloudi was included in France's squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|date=2 October 2019|title=La liste des joueurs pour la Coupe du Monde dévoilée!|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/actualite-a-la-une/la-liste-des-joueurs-pour-la-coupe-du-monde-devoilee/|publisher=FFRXIII|access-date=1 November 2019|language=French}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2019|title=XIII de France: Romain Puso remplace Lilian Albert, forfait!|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/actualite-a-la-une/xiii-de-france-romain-puso-remplace-lilian-albert-forfait/|publisher=FFR XIII|access-date=1 November 2019|language=French}}</ref>

In 2023 he played [[rugby union]] for [[Algeria national rugby union team|Algeria]] in the [[2023 Africa Men's Sevens]]; Algeria won the competition.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Jun 2023|accessdate=30 Jun 2023|title=Hakim Miloudi on Olympics qualification quest with Algeria and potential England return|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hakim-miloudi-olympics-algeria-potential-england-return}}</ref>
